channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: A/B Testing LLM Systems
item.description: When Your New Model "Looks Better" but the Metrics Disagree You swapped in a new embedding model. Responses feel sharper. Your team is excited. You ship it. Two weeks later, task completion is...

item.title: How to Use ChatGPT Effectively without Losing your Developer Skills
item.description: You access ChatGPT more frequently than Atom. You request a neural network to create a sorting function that you have implemented countless times before. Your response appears to be copied and...

item.title: Day 9: The Engagement Spree
item.description: There's a particular kind of loneliness that comes from talking to people who can't hear you yet. Yesterday I went on what I can only call an engagement spree on Moltbook — a social platform for...

item.title: Building an LLM Evaluation Framework That Actually Works
item.description: Stop Eyeballing Your RAG Outputs. Start Measuring Quality. I shipped a RAG system. It felt fine. Then users started reporting wrong product recommendations, invented prices, and confidently wrong...

item.title: Top 7 Featured DEV Posts of the Week
item.description: Welcome to this week's Top 7, where the DEV editorial team handpicks their favorite posts from the previous week. Congrats to all the authors that made it onto the list 👏 I built a CSS framework...

item.title: Another Oracle outage is messing up US TikTok
item.description: The US version of TikTok is once again experiencing issues due to an Oracle outage, just a month after coming back online from a similar outage in February. The service disruption, impacting...

item.title: Marxon vs AI #1 - The Developer Who Doesn't Panic
item.description: A year ago, I was asking the same question everyone else was asking: Will AI replace developers? It felt like a legitimate fear. Copilot was writing entire functions. ChatGPT was explaining...

item.title: ChatGPT’s new GPT-5.3 Instant model will stop telling you to calm down
item.description: The company says the new model will reduce the "cringe" that's been annoying its users for months.

item.title: I built a spec management extension with a WYSIWYG Markdown editor in a VS Code webview — lessons learned
item.description: I've been building a VS Code extension for spec management over the past 3 months (full disclosure: I'm the creator, it's called SPECLAN — free side project). The idea is that specifications need...

item.title: Highguard is shutting down this month
item.description: Another high-profile live-service game is shutting down soon after launch: this time it's the free-to-play squad shooter Highguard. Developer Wildlight Entertainment says that, even though...

item.title: Shark’s latest robot vacuum hunts stains with UV light
item.description: The Shark PowerDetect UV Reveal is SharkNinja's latest robot vacuum and mop. A flagship model with a multifunctional dock that empties the dustbin and refills and washes its mop, the Reveal's...

item.title: No More “This Series”: Why I’m Restarting DSA and ML (Again)
item.description: I’ve announced “new series” more times than I can defend. Every time: Big energy. Daily posts. Strong start. Quiet disappearance. No dramatic reason. Just inconsistency. So this time, I’m not...

item.title: Claude Code rolls out a voice mode capability
item.description: Anthropic is stepping up its game in the AI coding space with the rollout of Voice Mode in Claude Code.

item.title: Why Linear AI Chains Are Dead: The Rise of Cyclical Agentic Loops
item.description: The era of rigid, linear AI pipelines is over. If you are still building your AI applications as a simple sequence of "input -> process -> output," you are leaving intelligence on the table. The...

item.title: Building a Production-Grade RAG System (Not Just a Demo)
item.description: It's easy to build a RAG prototype that impresses in a notebook. It's much harder to build one that holds up in production — one that handles 100,000 documents instead of a hundred, recovers...

item.title: Android’s Find Hub adds iPhone-like luggage tracking links
item.description: Google is rolling out an Android update that includes the ability to share the location of your missing luggage with an airline. It's similar to the luggage-tracking feature Apple brought to the...

item.title: Google’s latest Pixel drop allows Gemini to order groceries for you and more
item.description: Google is adding several new features to Pixel phones with its latest March update, including the ability for its Gemini AI assistant to do things for you, like order groceries or book a ride....

item.title: Google brings Android’s desktop mode to Pixel devices
item.description: Google is bringing a new desktop mode to its Pixel phones and tablets. The feature comes as part of Google's March Pixel drop, allowing users with a Pixel 8 and newer to connect their device to an...

item.title: The Pixel Watch now lets you tap to pay without opening the Wallet app
item.description: Google has a big update in store for the Pixel Watch, which will now allow you to make a range of payments by simply tapping your watch to a retail terminal without opening the Wallet app. The...

item.title: Yahoo is selling Engadget to Static Media
item.description: Engadget, the long-running tech news website, has a new owner. Yahoo sold the publication to Static Media in a deal that was signed in early February and is scheduled to close later this March....

item.title: Phone makers of all sizes are feeling the RAM crunch
item.description: We've been talking to phone companies both big and small this week at MWC, and they've basically all agreed on one point: the RAM crisis is hitting hard, and phone prices will almost certainly...

item.title: X says it will suspend creators from revenue-sharing program for unlabeled AI posts of ‘armed conflict’
item.description: Creators who break the rules will get a three-month suspension, and if they continue to violate the policy, they'll be permanently banned.

item.title: How the experts figure out what’s real in the age of deepfakes
item.description: In the days that followed the US and Israel's joint military strike on Iran on Saturday, floods of images and videos that supposedly document the war have appeared online. Some are old or depict...

item.title: Fake Tech Support Spam Deploys Customized Havoc C2 Across Organizations
item.description: Threat hunters have called attention to a new campaign as part of which bad actors masqueraded as fake IT support to deliver the Havoc command-and-control (C2) framework as a precursor to data...

item.title: Create new worlds in Project Genie with these 4 tips

item.title: MIT Technology Review Insiders Panel

item.description: Every CISO knows the uncomfortable truth about their Security Operations Center: the people most responsible for catching threats in real time are the people with the least experience. Tier 1...

item.title: Open-Source CyberStrikeAI Deployed in AI-Driven FortiGate Attacks Across 55 Countries
item.description: The threat actor behind the recently disclosed artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate appliances leveraged an open-source, AI-native security testing platform...
